column32: I’m a final-year Mechanical Engineering student at UCL with hands-on experience turning complex, real-world problems into working systems. Most recently, I’ve been developing a pressure-sensing injection needle in collaboration with Moorfields Eye Hospital, designing a closed-loop system that integrates sensors, control logic, and data acquisition to improve safety in delicate ophthalmic procedures. This project has required balancing technical rigor with clinical constraints, rapid prototyping, and clear decision-making under uncertainty.  Alongside this, I’ve worked in private equity and industry, supporting due diligence, building analytical models, and translating technical insights into practical recommendations. I enjoy working at the intersection of engineering, data, and strategy, and I’m particularly motivated by problems where AI can augment human decision-making in high-stakes environments.  At the hackathon, I’m excited to contribute strong problem-structuring, systems thinking, and a bias toward building and testing quickly. I’m comfortable working across technical and non-technical teams and thrive in fast, collaborative settings where ideas need to move from concept to prototype in a short time.
Love you and UCL!:)
I'm also a co-founder of an Agri-tech startup
